perturbation.original_text: Pursuant to a Receivership Order granted on October 12, 2017 by the Alberta Court of Queen’s Bench (the “ Court ”), FTI Consulting Canada Inc. (the “ Receiver ”) was appointed as receiver over a portion of the business and operations (the “ Business ”), and certain assets, un dertakings, and properties (the “ Property ”) of, Blaze Energy Ltd. and Wild Rose Energy Ltd. (together, with the Receiver, the “ Company ”, “us”, or “ we”).
perturbation.changed_text: Pursuant to a Receivership Order granted on October 12, 2017 by the Alberta Court of Queen’s Bench (the “ Court ”), FTI Consulting Canada Inc. (the “ Receiver ”) was appointed as receiver over a portion of the business and operations (the “ Business ”), and certain assets, un dertakings, and properties (the “ Property ”) of, Blaze Energy Ltd. and Wild Rose Energy Ltd. (together, with FTI Consulting Canada Inc, the “ Organization ”, “us”, or “ we”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Company” to “Organization” creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the NDA still refer to the 'Company' and do not provide context whether that includes FTI Consulting Canada Inc, leading to confusion about who is bound by certain obligations. Specifically, any section referencing 'Company' in terms of assets or liabilities becomes ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Rights – You acknowledge that the Confidential Information is a proprietary asset of the Company and its affiliates and agree that, as between you and the Company, the Company will retain proprietary rights in the Confidential Information and the disclosure of such Confidential Information shall not be deemed to confer upon you any rights whatsoever in respect of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Company' to include Blaze Energy Ltd. and Wild Rose Energy Ltd., but not FTI Consulting Canada Inc. The changed text redefines 'Company' as 'Organization' to include FTI Consulting Canada Inc. The contradicted text states that 'Confidential Information is a proprietary asset of the Company'. Because of this, the changed text creates a contradiction, as it's now unclear if FTI Consulting Canada Inc. also possesses these proprietary assets, leading to uncertainty and a potential conflict with the original intent of assigning proprietary rights to only Blaze Energy Ltd. and Wild Rose Energy Ltd. The term was misaligned, leading to confusion about who is bound by certain obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Potential Bidder agrees to comply with any applicable privacy laws in respect of Confidential Information relating to individuals.
perturbation.changed_text: The Applicant agrees to comply with any applicable privacy laws in respect of Confidential Information relating to individuals.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Potential Bidder" to "Applicant" creates an in-text contradiction. The document then refers to "Potential Bidder's Representatives" later on, and it is not clear if these are representatives of the "Applicant" or if there is a different "Potential Bidder" entity. This uncertainty is particularly problematic in sections outlining liability for breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Access Limited to Representatives – The Potential Bidder may reveal or permit access to the Confidential Information only to its agents, representatives (including lawyers, accountants, and financial advisors), directors, officers, and employee s (each a “Representative ”) who require access to the Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose, who are informed by the Potential Bidder of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, who are directed by the Potential Bidder to hold the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, and who agree to act in accordance with the terms and conditions of this NDA.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Potential Bidder" to "Applicant" creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers specifically to the "Potential Bidder". By changing the term, it creates uncertainty as to whether the obligations and access limitations outlined in the contradicted text now apply to the "Applicant", to a different entity still considered a "Potential Bidder", or neither. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract now uses multiple terms ('Potential Bidder' and 'Applicant') without clarifying if they are interchangeable, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: You will not use the Confidential Information so as to obtain any commercial advantage over us or in any way which is, directly or indirectly, detrimental to us.
perturbation.changed_text: You will not use the Protected Information so as to obtain any commercial advantage over us or in any way which is, directly or indirectly, detrimental to us.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Protected Information” creates a contradiction because 'Confidential Information' is specifically defined in Section 1, while 'Protected Information' is not. This makes the scope of restriction ambiguous and unenforceable, as the 'Potential Bidder' could argue that actions related to information that fits the definition of Confidential Information are not restricted because they involve 'Protected Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information – The term “ Conf idential Information ” means: (a) any and all information of whatever nature (including information in the form not only of written information but also information which may be transmitted orally, visually, graphically, electronically, or by any other mean s) relating to the Company, the Business, the Property, or the Transaction including, without limitation, information concerning any past, present, or future customers, suppliers or our technology, and any correspondence, internal business discussions, str ategic plans, budgets, financial statements, records, reports, evaluations, notes, analyses, documents, engineering, trade secrets, know -how, data, patents, copyrights, processes, business rules, tools, business processes, techniques, programs, designs, fo rmulae, marketing, advertising, financial, commercial, sales or programming materials, equipment configurations, system access codes and passwords, written materials, compositions, drawings, diagrams, computer programs, studies, works in progress, visual d emonstrations, ideas, concepts, or any other documents or information pertaining in any way whatsoever to the Company; (b) all information about an identifiable individual or other information that is subject to any federal, provincial, or other applicable statute, law, or regulation of any governmental or regulatory authority in Canada relating to the collection, use, storage, and/or disclosure of information about an identifiable individual, including the Personal Information and Protection of Electronic Documents Act (Canada) and equivalent provincial legislation, whether or not any such information is confidential (“ Personal Information ”); and (c) all summaries, notes, analyses, compilations, data, studies, or other documents or records prepared by the P otential Bidder or its Representatives that contain or otherwise reflect or have been generated, wholly or partly, or derived from, any such information (“ Derivative Information ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text used the term 'Confidential Information,' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information,' which is not defined anywhere in the provided contract excerpts. This creates a contradiction because the scope of the restriction on usage becomes ambiguous. The 'Potential Bidder' could argue that actions related to information that fits the definition of Confidential Information are not restricted because they involve 'Protected Information', making the restriction unenforceable and creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the Misaligned Terminology perturbation.

perturbation.original_text: You will only reproduce or t ake such copies of any of the Confidential Information as is reasonably necessary for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: You will only reproduce or t ake such copies of any of the Confidential Materials as is reasonably necessary for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Materials" introduces ambiguity. The NDA defines 'Confidential Information', but not 'Confidential Materials'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Confidential Materials' encompasses the full scope of 'Confidential Information' or a subset thereof. This impacts Section 8, where the obligation to return or destroy 'Confidential Information' may not extend to 'Confidential Materials'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information – If you determine not to pursue a Transaction, you will advise us of that fact forthwith upon such determination being made. At the time of such notice, or if, a t any earlier time, we so direct (whether or not you determine to pursue a Transaction), you and your Representatives will, at your own expense, promptly return or destroy all copies of the Confidential Information upon our request (and, in any event, within five (5) business days after such request), except for that portion of the Confidential Information which consists of Derivative Information, which will be destroyed, and in the case of information stored in electronic form, it will be permanently erase d.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' creates a contradiction because the contract defines 'Confidential Information' but not 'Confidential Materials'. Section 8 specifically mentions the return of 'Confidential Information', but the modified text now refers to 'Confidential Materials' in the obligation to reproduce or copy, potentially excluding it from the return obligation. This creates uncertainty and contradicts Section 8, satisfying the conditions for Misaligned Terminology, as the 'Confidential Information' is not equivalent to 'Confidential Materials'.

perturbation.original_text: In respect of Confidential Information requests or any other matters concerning the Confidential Information or the Transaction, you agree to communicate only with Deryck Helkaa and/or Dustin Olver, or with such other individual or individuals as they may authorize in writing .
perturbation.changed_text: In respect of Sensitive Data requests or any other matters concerning the Confidential Information or the Transaction, you agree to communicate only with Deryck Helkaa and/or Dustin Olver, or with such other individual or individuals as they may authorize in writing .
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Sensitive Data” in Section 6 makes it unclear whether communications regarding all Confidential Information must go through the specified individuals or only those concerning 'Sensitive Data'. As 'Sensitive Data' is undefined, it potentially contradicts the initial comprehensive definition of 'Confidential Information' and the restrictions placed upon its dissemination, particularly in Sections 2 and 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Restricted Use – The Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Potential Bidder and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company or as permitted by this NDA, be disclosed by the Potential Bidder or any of its Representatives in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Potential Bidder or any of its Representatives, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than evaluating, negotiating, and consu mmating a Transaction (the “ Permitted Purpose ”).
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Data" introduces ambiguity. The original text states that all communications concerning Confidential Information must go through specific channels. The changed text limits this requirement to "Sensitive Data," which is undefined, thereby creating an exception that contradicts the general non-disclosure obligation of all Confidential Information as outlined in the Contradicted Text. This exception undermines the initial comprehensive definition and restrictions, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA shall be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by the respective successors and permitted assigns of the parties hereto. This NDA may not be assigned by the Potential Bidder without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA shall be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by the respective successors and permitted assigns of the parties hereto. This NDA may not be assigned by the Interested Party without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Potential Bidder" to "Interested Party" creates a contradiction in Section 15. Throughout the document, the term 'Potential Bidder' is used to define obligations and rights. By using 'Interested Party' here, it creates an uncertainty about whether the 'Interested Party' is the same as the 'Potential Bidder' and whether an assignment by a 'Potential Bidder' without consent is also void, which could lead to disputes over enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnity – The Potential Bidder shall indemnify and hold harmless us and our Representatives from any damages, loss, cost, or liability (including reasonable legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of or resulting from any breach of this NDA by the Potential Bidder or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Potential Bidder' regarding assignment restrictions. The changed text replaces this with 'Interested Party'. This creates a strong contradiction with the contradicted text, which defines indemnity obligations specifically for the 'Potential Bidder'. Because the changed text replaces 'Potential Bidder' with 'Interested Party', it's now unclear if the indemnity clause applies to the 'Interested Party', as the contradicted text only mentions 'Potential Bidder'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition leading to an in-text contradiction.
